description: Cost-optimization epic, candidate #4 (docs/prd/cost-optimization-candidates.md §2, Claude-contributed). BYTE-IDENTICAL to candidate #3 (multiagent-aggressive-haiku) in structure and in EVERY prompt -- same Sonnet coordinator, same four sub-agents, same skill-scoped system prompts, same task prompt -- with EXACTLY ONE difference: every sub-agent runs on Sonnet 5 (research, selection, writing, and listening-script), not Haiku. Its purpose is to isolate the STRUCTURAL lever from the model lever: the original cost analysis attributed the dominant cost to repeated full-context replay across one long agentic session (the post-research writing phase alone re-sent ~4.2M cache-read tokens), which is a STRUCTURAL cause, not a model one. This candidate breaks that one long session into four fresh-context sub-agents that each pick up only their predecessor's output file from the shared /workspace working folder rather than the whole accumulated transcript -- testing whether decomposition ALONE (no cheaper model anywhere) recovers most of the savings while holding quality essentially constant (all Sonnet). Read #3 vs #4 to isolate 'did the Haiku-on-research+write+script lever help/hurt', and #4 vs production-baseline to isolate 'did the decomposition structure alone cut cost'. Content generation ONLY: holds no AWS/delivery role (FR-1), never fans out to subscribers, never synthesizes audio (no TTS in evals, owner-confirmed 2026-07-06); its only delivery-side contact is the read-only GET /recent-briefs route. The coordinator and each sub-agent are SEPARATE, newly-created agent resources (their own agent_ids, written once at first sync). A-VERIFICATION ITEM: the exact multi-agent execution semantics -- how the coordinator delegates and whether sub-agents share the /workspace working folder for artifact passing -- is verified in step A (eval-harness re-integration) and may refine the sub-agent delegation wording, but not the models/structure/skill-scoping declared here.
schedule_intent: none -- cost-optimization eval candidate only; never scheduled and never promoted to production without a separate, explicitly-approved cut-over decision
